title: Aeschynomene spinulosa
intro: Aeschynomene spinulosa, also known as spiny horse bean or hairy cowpea, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The leaves and stems of Aeschynomene spinulosa have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Aeschynomene spinulosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Aeschynomene spinulosa is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is a part of the genus Aeschynomene and is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Aeschynomene spinulosa is further classified into the subfamily Faboideae. The plant has been classified into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. Aeschynomene spinulosa also falls under the class Magnoliopsida and the order Fabales.
